COMMISSIONERS OF REVENUE AND CIRCUIT FOR DISTRICTS.

Agra, Muttra, Furruckabad, Mynpoorie, Etawah—J. H. Batten.
Allahabad, Futtehpoore, Cawnpore, Banda—C. B. Thornhill.
Bareilly, Shahjehanpore, Moradabad, Bijnore, Budaon—W. Roberts.
Benares, Mirzapore, Jaunpore, Goruckpore, Ghazeepeore, Azimghur—A. Shake-
spear.
Bhaugulpore, Monghyr, Purneah, Tirhoot—A. Money, C.B.
Burdwan, Hooghly (including Magistracy of Howrah), Bankorah, Beerbhoom,
and Midnapore, G. A. C. Plowden.
Chittagong, Tipperah, Bulloah—H. M. Reid.
Cuttack, Balasore, Pooree—R. N. Shore.
Dacca, Mymensing, Sylhet, Backergunge, Furreedpore—C. T. Buckland.
Delhi, Goorgaon—
Hissar, Rohtuck, Jhujjen, and Sirsa—P. S. Melvill.
Jhansi, Jaloun, Humeerpore, and Chundeyree—Capt. F. W. Pinkney, M.E.
Nuddeah, 24 Pergunnahs, including Calcutta, Baraset, Jessore, and Sunderbunds
—V. H. Schalch.
Meerut, Mozzuffurnugger, Saharunpore, Boolundshuhur, Deyra Dhoon, Allygurh
—F. Williams.
Patna, Behar, Sarun, Shahabad, Champarun—G. F. Cockburn.
Rajshahye, Pubna, Rungpore, Bograh, Dinagepore, Moorshedabad, and Malda

ORDER OF THE STAR OF INDIA,

INSTITUTED 1861,

Consisting of the Sovereign, the Grand Master, and twenty-five Knights.

SOVEREIGN.

Her Most Gracious Majesty the QUEEN.

GRAND MASTER.

Right Hon. the EARL of ELGIN and KINCARDINE, K.T., G.C.B.,
Viceroy, and Governor General of India.

ORDER OF THE BATH.

Officers of Her Majesty's Indian Services who are Members of the Most Honourable Order of the Bath, with the dates of their appointments.

* Signifies retired from the service; B. Bengal; M. Madras; Bo. Bombay.

MILITARY KNIGHTS GRAND CROSS.

Caldwell, Gen. Sir J. L., M., 30 Aug. 41 | Pollock, Gen. Sir G., B., 2 Dec. 42

CIVIL KNIGHTS GRAND CROSS.

M'Neill,* Rt. Hon. Sir J., Bo., 15 April 39 | Lawrence,* Rt. Hon. Sir J. L. M., Lt., B., 11 Nov. 57.

No. of old
Cadre.		Names.Date of present Commissions.Date of
present ap-
pointment.		Remarks.
Staff Corps.Army.

Brigades of Royal Artillery.

Two Brigades of H. Artillery (2nd and 5th R.A.), 5 Brigades of Foot Artillery (16th, 19th, 22nd, 24th, and 25th R.A.) The 2nd Brigade consists of 7 Troops, and the 5th Brigade of 6 Troops. The 16th, 19th, 22nd, and 24th Brigades consist each of 5 Companies, and the 25th Brigade of 4 Companies.

The Royal Arms and Supporters, with a Cannon. "Ubique" over the Gun, and "Quo Fas et Gloria ducunt" below it. Blue; Facings Scarlet, Lace Gold.

* G.C.B. † K.C.B. ‡ C.B. || Supernumeraries.

Grant, G.C.B., Commander-in-Chief,
took his seat 26 Dec. 61

seat 16th Dec. 59

LAW DEPARTMENT.HIGH COURT OF JUDICATURE.

ESTABLISHED 1862.

Chief Justice—Hon. Sir C. H. Scotland, Knt.Judges—Hon. Sir Adam Bittleston, Knt., Henry Dominic Phillips,
Hatley Frere, and William Holloway, Esqrs.LAW OFFICERS.Advocate General—T. S. Smyth, 1853Solicitor—J. R. Boyson, 1857OFFICERS OF THE COURT.Sheriff—Col. J. Impett.Deputy Sheriff—E. ShawMaster in Equity—C. M. TeedAccountant General—F. LushingtonRegistrars—C. F. Chamier, A. M. RitchieClerk of the Crown—J. B. NortonDeputy Clerk of the Crown and Examiner—B. BrookesCoroner—J. et Gloria ducunt" below it.

3rd Brigade of Royal
Horse Artillery... }		 Bangalore. Arrived 23rd Oct. 38
17th Brigade Royal
Foot Artillery... }		 Mount. Do. 13th Apr. 57
20th Brigade " Kamptee. Do. 30th Jan. 57
23rd Brigade " Secunderabad Do. 10th May 61

One Brigade of Horse Artillery, consisting of Four Troops; three Brigades of Foot Artillery, one of six Companies, and two of five.

Corps of Sappers and Miners.

"Seringapatam"—"Java"—"Egypt"—"Assaye"—"Mahidpore"—"Nagpore"—
"Ava."

A, B, C, and E Companies, "Pegu."

A, B, and F Companies bear, on their appointments, a Dragon, wearing an Imperial Crown, with the word "China."

C Company bears on its appointments "Meeanee" and "Hyderabad, 1848."

BOMBAY.

SIR HENRY BARTLE EDWARD FRERE, K.C.B.,

GOVERNOR.

TOOK HIS SEAT 24TH APRIL, 1862.

COUNCIL.

Member, Lieut.-Gen. Sir William Mansfield, K.C.B., Commander-in-Chief, took his seat 29th June, 1860.

Member, William Edward Frere, took his seat 7th April, 1860.

Member, Jonathan Duncan Inverarity, took his seat 24th March, 1862.

LAW DEPARTMENT.HIGH COURT OF JUDICATURE.

(Established 1862.)

Chief Justice, Hon. Sir Matthew Richard Sausse, Kt.Judges, Hon. Sir Joseph Arnould, Kt.; C. J. Erskine, A. K. Forbes, Richard Couch, H. Newton, and A. B. IRREGULAR REGIMENTS.

Ahmedabad.] Guzerat Irregular Horse—(Civil).

Facings white, lace gold.

Commandant—Captain J. Harpur, Staff Corps

Second in command—Lieutenant P. H. Le Geyt, Staff Corps

Adjutant—

Medical charge—

Candeish Bheel Corps.

Jacket dark blue, lace gold.

Dhurrungam.]

Commandant—Captain O. Probyn, Inv. Est., 17 Oct. 60

Second in command—Capt. S. Scott, Staff Corps, 29 Aug. 62

Adjutant—Lieutenant H. S. Daniell, 3 d Light Cav., 22 Oct. 62

Medical charge—B. Burn (Apoth.)

Rutnagerry.]

Rutnagerry Rangers.

Commandant—Lieutenant S. McGillivray, Staff Corps, 16 Mar. 63

Guzerat Bheel Corps.

Green, facings red.

Commandant—Captain R. M. Bonner, Staff Corps, 10 Aug. 59—on furl.

Adjutant—

Kaira.]

Guzerat Police Corps.

Facings light yellow, lace gold.

Commandant—Capt. R. Johnstone, Staff Corps, 28 May 62

Ahmedabad.]

Guzerat Cooly Police Corps.

Commandant—Capt. T. Thatcher, Staff Corps, 20 Mar. 61

Assistant—Capt. T. G. Coles, Staff Corps, 5 Nov. 61

Tanna.]

Tanna Police Corps.

Commandant—Major A. B. Hodgson, Staff Corps, 7 Apr. 62

Sawunt Warree Local Corps.

Commandant—Major F. Schneider, Staff Corps, 10 Aug. 59

Second in command—Captain A. C. Way, Staff Corps, 10 Aug. 59—on surl.

Adjutant—Lieutenant G. R. C. Westropp, Staff Corps, 10 Aug. 59

Sattara Local Corps.

Commandant—Major C. W. Walker, Staff Corps, 19 Sept. 62

Assistant—Lieut. E. P. Bartholomew, Staff Corps, 5 Nov. 61

Kolapore.]

Kolapore Infantry Corps.

Commandant—Major M. J. Soppitt, 12th N. I., 6 Jan. 62

Adjutant—Lieutenant T. Kettlewell, Staff Corps, 29 Nov. 58

Medical Charge—Assistant Surgeon J. Mills
